channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: connpool: A Zero-Alloc TCP Connection Pool for Go
item.description: The Story Years ago, when I first needed TCP connection pooling in Go, I found fatih/pool. It was elegant, simple, and it taught me a lot about channel-based pooling design. It was a real...

item.title: Build for Consumability, The Provider to Consumer Model That Makes AWS Scale.
item.description: Most platform teams think their job is to build infrastructure. They're wrong. Their job is to build infrastructure that other teams can consume without thinking about it. The difference matters...

item.title: Why Erlang C Always Understaffs Your Call Center (And What to Use Instead)
item.description: A.K. Erlang published his queuing formula in 1917 to figure out how many telephone circuits the Copenhagen Telephone Company needed. Over a century later, call center managers still plug three...

item.title: 5 AI Coding Mistakes That Waste Hours (and the Prompts That Fix Them)
item.description: You paste code into ChatGPT. You get an answer. It looks right. You ship it. Two hours later, it breaks. I've made every AI coding mistake in the book. Here are the five that cost me the most time...

item.title: 🚀 The Future is Now: AI, 6G & Space Tech.
item.description: 👋 Hey everyone! I’m Mazeed — telecom engineer turned AI & space tech enthusiast. I’m obsessed with pushing the boundaries of technology, from next-gen networks to AI-driven space systems. This...

item.title: The Power of Simple Tools: Why Sometimes All You Need is a Counter
item.description: As developers and tech enthusiasts, we often get caught up in the "feature race." We build complex systems, integrate AI into everything, and obsess over syncing data across every possible cloud....

item.title: The Lazy Developer's Guide to Perfect Social Media Previews
item.description: You finally ship your project. You share the link on Twitter. And the preview looks like... nothing. A blank card. Or worse, some random text fragment from your footer. I've been there. Multiple...

item.title: Evaluate LLM code generation with LLM-as-judge evaluators
item.description: Which AI model writes the best code for your codebase? Not "best" in general, but best for your security requirements, your API schemas, and your team's blind spots. This tutorial shows you how to...

item.title: Lean Caching - A Custom NetworkBoundResource in Kotlin
item.description: Orchestrating data between a remote API and a local database is a foundational problem in mobile development. While the industry consensus rightly leans toward the Single Source of Truth pattern,...

item.title: What Happens to Your Calls After 5 PM? An Honest Cost Comparison
item.description: It's 6:17 PM. A prospect who's been thinking about your product all day finally has time to call. They've been on your website, read the comparison page, they're ready to talk to someone. They...

item.title: Govee’s color-changing smart lamps are on sale for up to 30 percent off
item.description: Speaking from experience, better lighting can completely change how a space feels. Over the years, I’ve filled my apartment with Govee’s color-changing lights, and they’ve made it feel calmer and...

item.title: Meta gets ready to launch two new Ray-Ban AI glasses
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. Meta and its AI glasses...

item.title: Transform your headphones into a live personal translator on iOS.
item.description: Google Translate’s Live translate with headphones is officially arriving on iOS! And we're expanding the capability for both iOS and Android users to even more countries…

item.title: Everyone hates Ticketmaster. Why’d Trump go easy on them?
item.description: Today on Decoder, we’re talking about the major antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation, and what it might mean for antitrust and competition law in general now that the Justice Department under...

item.title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
item.description: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
item.description: The Gemini emblem sits next to text reading 'Gemini 3.1 Flash Live'. The background has blue, multicolored dots making up a microphone icon

item.title: Wikipedia bans AI-generated articles
item.description: Wikipedia will no longer allow editors to write or rewrite articles using AI. The update, which was added to Wikipedia's guidelines late last week, cites the tendency for AI-written articles to...

item.title: The best deals we’ve found from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale (so far)
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale, which starts today and runs through March 31st, the retail...

item.title: Search Live is expanding globally
item.description: A graphic with the words Search Live shown underneath a waveform icon. To the right, a phone shows the Google app with Search Live open. The camera is pointing at trees in a forest.

item.title: Senators are pushing to find out how much electricity data centers actually use
item.description: On Thursday, senators Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Josh Hawley (R-MO) sent a letter to the Energy Information Administration (EIA) asking it to collect "comprehensive, annual energy-use...

item.title: Why a two-seater robotaxi makes more sense than you think
item.description: When Tesla revealed the Cybercab in 2024, many people were baffled by the automaker's decision to make it a two-seater. I had similar thoughts when I first saw it at the LA Auto Show later that...

item.title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
item.description: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

item.title: Cohere launches an open source voice model specifically for transcription
item.description: Relatively light at just 2 billion parameters, the model is meant for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host it. It currently supports 14 languages.

item.title: [Webinar] Stop Guessing. Learn to Validate Your Defenses Against Real Attacks
item.description: Most teams have security tools in place. Alerts are firing, dashboards look clean, threat intel is flowing in. On the surface, everything feels under control. But one question usually stays...

item.title: Claude Extension Flaw Enabled Zero-Click XSS Prompt Injection via Any Website
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Google Chrome Extension that could have been exploited to trigger malicious prompts simply by visiting a web page....

item.title: Some of our favorite Apple tech is cheaper than ever during Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: It feels like nearly everything is receiving some sort of discount as part of Amazon’s Big Spring Sale — that is, except for most Apple devices. Don’t worry, though; you’re not completely out of...

item.title: Webtoon is adding AI localization tools to its comics platform
item.description: Canvas, Webtoon's platform for user-uploaded comics, is about to get a major overhaul that's designed to help creators make more money and share their art with a wider audience. Today, Webtoon...

item.title: The versatile Play speaker is a great way into the Sonos world
item.description: It's been nearly a year and a half since Sonos released a consumer product, and those last two releases - the Arc Ultra and Sub 4 - were updates to existing products. The $299 Sonos Play, however,...

item.title: The Download: a battery pivot to AI, and rewriting math
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why this battery company is pivoting to AI Qichao Hu...

item.title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
item.description: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.
